results.rendered_html: 2. ayuta (3. a + yuta)  ŚĀNT. 3,7 . eine Myriade  NIR. 3,10 . m.n.  H. 873 .  SIDDH. K. 250,b,10 . sahasraṃ savā~ ayutaṃ ca sākam  ṚV. 3,6,15 . na sahasrāya nāyutāya vajrivo na śatāya 8,1,5 . 2,41 . 21,18 . 34,15 . 46,22 .  VS. 17,2 .  AV. 8,2,21 . 8,7 . 10,8,24 .  ŚAT. BR. 13,5,4,8 .  KĀTY. ŚR. 23,1,9 . 22,11,6 . ajñānām  M. 12,113 .  MATSYOP. 4 .  R. 5,47,12 . 73,10 . 6,13,17 . varṣāyuta  N. 26,27 .  R. 3,21,7 . vṛkṣānpuṣpitānvihagāyutān 5,95,14 . Das m. nicht zu belegen. Am Anf. eines comp. gaṇa kāṣṭhādi.

results.rendered_html:
ayuta a.
1 Disjoined, detached, not connected.
2 Uninterrupted, undisturbed (Ved.)
— taṃ Ten thousand, a myriad.
Comp.
— adhyāpakaḥ a good teacher.
— siddha a. (in  Vaiś.  phil.) proved to be inseparable and inherent.
— siddhiḥ f. proof that certain things or notions are inseparable and inherent.
— homa a kind of sacrifice.
